summary: No regression risk was identified for this design-input judgment change on current HEAD. The phase split, authorization risk propagation, and Brainbase/import/gate surfaces remain separated and wired consistently, and focused checks cover both design-input and pre-implementation paths.

inspection.summary: Reviewed regression risk and surface coverage for design-input vs pre-implementation behavior across diagnosis execution, PR context construction, Brainbase import selection, repo status run selection, CLI surface entry points, and existing test coverage. Verified key tests pass with current HEAD.

judgment_delta: Initial concern: phase split and legacy paths may regress by merging design-input/pre-implementation evidence, especially authorization_order_risks and Brainbase import provenance -> Final: pass because run/build paths retain explicit phase-specific judgment fields, pre-implementation still defaults via resolve/normalize, and tests verify risk preservation plus path isolation on both surfaces.

Initial concern: repo status/runtime/cost/gate visibility might drift from current-head artifacts -> Final: pass because status chooses selected story run evidence deterministically and head-anchoring gates/verification evidence checks use explicit current-head/manifest/runtime bindings where required, with no new unbound read path introduced in this commit.
